subject: Re: (no subject)
body:
Jerffrey, what happened to you? I thought that the seventh was important. Good news?
I think you didn't quite understand what Pilar said, I have never sent a bill for all the things that I have worked on, but that doesn't mean that I have done nothing as you often say.
I do agree that is an excellent idea that someone who understand all this design process can help giving a clear view on where we stand, it is obvious that we do not communicate. Even your last mail, with copy to your lawyer, makes me guilty of not putting a bedroom in the New Library, which you and I had eliminated, and anyone would know how easy would be to re-denominate the vestibule next to the bathroom as a bedroom... In any case that is what McGuire did on all the other drawings. I ?was expecting perhaps a comment on the idea of keeping an office on the second floor,etc.
Regarding your question to Pilar who did what in Canada, is the same set up that I thought we had, I did the design and a Canadian firm did the construction and all the permitting, the engineering etc. (the equivalent to McGuire, with the difference that they could not alter my design with out my approval, I don't know if you have seen their version of your office building, is rather awful)
I will call you tomorrow evening, if it is not to late, if not Tuesday early morning. J.P.


-----Original Message-----
From: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
To: Jpm6929@aol.com
Sent: Sun, 9 Mar 2008 8:18 pm
Subject: (no subject)



I just had a disturbing conversation with your wife,, i would appreciate a call, 
, she thought that I owed you money for spending time on guermante, hotel 
lambert, , the two projects of the architectural firm.. I suggested that we 
engage a third party to make an independent judgement , and she thought that was 
very unfair,

Jerffrey, what happened to you? I thought that the seventh was important. Good news?
I think you didn't quite understand what Pilar said, I have never sent a bill for all the things that I have worked on, but that doesn't mean that I have done nothing as you often say.
I do agree that is an excellent idea that someone who understand all this design process can help giving a clear view on where we stand, it is obvious that we do not communicate. Even your last mail, with copy to your lawyer, makes me guilty of not putting a bedroom in the New Library, which you and I had eliminated, and anyone would know how easy would be to re-denominate the vestibule next to the bathroom as a bedroom... In any case that is what McGuire did on all the other drawings. I  was expecting perhaps a comment on the idea of keeping an office on the second floor,etc.
Regarding your question to Pilar who did what in Canada, is the same set up that I thought we had, I did the design and a Canadian firm did the construction and all the permitting, the engineering etc. (the equivalent to McGuire, with the difference that they could not alter my design with out my approval, I don't know if you have seen their version of your office building, is rather awful)
I will call you tomorrow evening, if it is not to late, if not Tuesday early morning. J.P.
